name: ThreatNova
description: AI-driven threat intelligence platform that accelerates detection and enriches investigation context across hybrid environments.
insight: Helps security teams cut research time during active investigations by surfacing relevant threat context automatically.
whyItMatters: Context speed is critical when incidents are unfolding in real time — delayed intel means delayed response.
label: Featured

name: IdentityForge
description: Zero-trust identity verification engine built for distributed teams and hybrid cloud architectures.
insight: Bridges the gap between legacy IAM systems and modern zero-trust requirements without rip-and-replace.
whyItMatters: Identity is the new perimeter. Organizations that fail to modernize authentication are exposed to lateral movement attacks.
label: Featured

name: CloudVector
description: Real-time cloud security posture management with automated misconfiguration detection and compliance mapping.
insight: Catches the cloud misconfigs that attackers scan for first — S3 buckets, open security groups, excessive IAM permissions.
whyItMatters: Most cloud breaches start with misconfigurations, not sophisticated exploits. Posture management is baseline hygiene.
label: Featured

name: HuntGrid
description: Collaborative threat hunting workspace that connects SOC analysts with shared detection logic and hunt playbooks.
insight: Turns isolated hunting efforts into a shared knowledge base — one analyst's detection becomes the team's advantage.
whyItMatters: Threat hunting doesn't scale when every analyst starts from scratch. Shared context multiplies detection coverage.
label: Community Pick

name: SignalRoot
description: Open-source signal analysis framework for extracting indicators from unstructured threat data feeds and dark web sources.
insight: Automates the messy work of parsing IOCs from Telegram channels, paste sites, and underground forums.
whyItMatters: The best threat intel often lives in unstructured sources that traditional tools can't ingest efficiently.
label: Early Stage

name: AppSentri
description: Developer-first application security testing platform with inline code review, SAST, and dependency risk scoring.
insight: Shifts security left by embedding directly into IDE and CI/CD pipelines — developers fix issues before they ship.
whyItMatters: Security that lives outside the developer workflow gets ignored. Embedding it in the IDE changes adoption completely.

name: NodeTrace
description: Runtime application security monitoring for Node.js and serverless environments with real-time exploit detection.
insight: Catches zero-day exploitation attempts in production Node.js apps by monitoring runtime behavior, not just known signatures.
whyItMatters: Static analysis finds known patterns. Runtime monitoring catches the exploitation attempts that happen in production.
label: Community Pick

name: PhantomLayer
description: Deception-as-a-service platform deploying honeypots, decoy credentials, and tripwires across enterprise environments.
insight: Creates a minefield for attackers inside your network — every interaction with a decoy is a high-fidelity alert.
whyItMatters: Deception generates alerts with near-zero false positives. No legitimate user touches a honeypot.

name: ZeroDock
description: Container and Kubernetes security scanner with runtime policy enforcement and supply chain integrity validation.
insight: Validates container images before deployment and monitors runtime behavior for anomalies across K8s clusters.
whyItMatters: Container security is often an afterthought. Supply chain attacks through compromised images are rising fast.
label: Early Stage

name: BlueMesh Labs
description: Community-built open-source SOC automation toolkit with pre-built playbooks, integrations, and enrichment workflows.
insight: Gives lean SOC teams enterprise-grade automation capabilities without the enterprise price tag.
whyItMatters: Small and mid-size security teams deserve quality automation. Community-built tools close the resource gap.
label: Community Pick
